Document:
# NON-Modifiable Command SOP
## Operations
Operations are considered ended when the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] announces such, and it is their responsibility to inform all personnel.
Some examples of cases where an operation can be considered ended include:
- The [bold]AO[/bold] is safe and secure, personnel are no longer needed and may be relieved to rest. - Evacuation of the [bold]AO[/bold] has occured, with no intent to return.
## Forward Operating Base
The [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] in charge of an operation must take the minimum precautions to ensure security and defense for stationed personnel, and incoming transport, when designating a [bold]Forward Operating Base[/bold].
- The [bold]FOB[/bold] must be a [bold]Secure Area[/bold] and designated by the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color]. - The primary landing zone still requires its outlined defenses, regardless of where the [bold]FOB[/bold] is designated. - Only Bravo may be stationed as the primary landing zone and [bold]FOB[/bold]'s primary defense. Any additional squads may assist where needed, and combat technicians may also be ordered to help reinforce the [bold]FOB[/bold] and primary landing zone defenses before resuming their original squad's orders.
## Secure Areas
- Additional areas may be designated as a [bold]Secure Area[/bold]s by any [bold]CIC Personnel[/bold]. Their secure status may be revoked for any reason by any [bold]CIC Personnel[/bold]. It should be announced in both cases for clarity. - These areas dictate where certain shipside personnel are allowed to be given permission to work. - These areas are no longer secure if there is a breach or the area is being attacked by hostiles.
### Field Hospital
- It must be designated as a [bold]Secure Area[/bold], which in this case can be designated by the [bold]Chief Medical Officer[/bold] as well. - The Field Hospital itself must also be designated as such by the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] or [bold]Chief Medical Officer[/bold]. - This should be announced for clarity.
### Primary Landing Zone
- Selected for the entire duration of the operation by the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color]. - The primary landing zone must be reinforced with a minimum of a line of non-makeshift defenses, including stationed marines.
## Conduct of Briefing
The [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] is to ensure a proper briefing before any operation is being held and personnel deployed. - It may be skipped in the event of a code [bold][color=black]Delta[/color][/bold] or another emergency situation of the same severity. - A briefing should generally be completed before fueling is completed for an operation, unless there are circumstances to resolve that would delay it.
### Location
- Brief must be performed in an area where a proper inspection of personnel can be held and where personnel can hear any orders. - Areas that interfere with non-combat personnel must be avoided, for example the medbay.
### Personnel Requirements
- Briefings at minimum must include the Squad Leaders. - Briefings at minimum must include [bold]CIC Personnel[/bold], unless the [bold]CIC Personnel[/bold] are performing other essential roles at the time, and may be excused by the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] at their discretion. Notify the [color=#ff000d][bold]MP[/bold][/color] beforehand.
### Scouts and Intel
- Scout Specialists and Intelligence Squad members may deploy before the general briefing, if all members have a collective briefing of their orders and are ready to deploy.
## High Ranking Officers UNMC Officers ranked [bold]Major[/bold] or above but are not already the [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color] of the warship receive these rights: - The same arrest immunities normally granted to the warship's [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color]. - The right to accept being appointed as the warship [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color] by the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color]. This does not also mean they have to be the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] of the current operation. - The right to assign themselves the [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color] position of the warship if the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] would need to be replaced as defined by "[textlink="Definitions" link="RMCMarineLawDefinitions"]", becoming the new [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] as well.
## Deputizing
When deputized, these personnel are expected and required to follow Marine Law like an [color=#ff000d][bold]MP[/bold][/color], and will carry the same punishments.
The [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] may deputize the following personnel to make arrests and enforce Marine Law, should [color=#ff000d][bold]MP[/bold][/color]s be unavailable or unable to respond in a timely manner. - The Executive Officer. - The Auxiliary Support Officer. - Staff Officers. - UNMC Synthetics. - Department Heads.
### Approval Required
- The [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color] may freely deputize under the above conditions. - Any other [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] must also obtain the approval of the current [color=#8B0000][bold]Chief MP[/bold][/color], unless it is not currently possible to assign an acting [color=#8B0000][bold]Chief MP[/bold][/color].
